findings.selector: html > body > main.container > div.features:nth-of-type(2) > div.feature:nth-of-type(1) > div.skeleton.sk-line:nth-of-type(1)
findings.common_name: Skeletons that don’t feel skeletal. / div
findings.title: Duration 1400ms exceeds the UI budget
findings.detail: 1400ms is over the 300ms ceiling for UI motion.
findings.why: Longer animations make the interface feel sluggish; a 180ms transition feels snappier than a 400ms one.
findings.fix: Reduce to ≤ 300ms (150–250ms is the sweet spot for most UI).

findings.selector: body > main.container > section.section > div.sk-card:nth-of-type(2) > div:nth-of-type(1) > div.skeleton.sk-avatar:nth-of-type(1)
findings.title: Duration 1400ms exceeds the UI budget
findings.detail: 1400ms is over the 300ms ceiling for UI motion.
findings.why: Longer animations make the interface feel sluggish; a 180ms transition feels snappier than a 400ms one.
findings.fix: Reduce to ≤ 300ms (150–250ms is the sweet spot for most UI).

findings.title: No prefers-reduced-motion path
findings.detail: The page animates movement (transform / keyframes) but ships no `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` rule.
findings.why: Motion-sensitive users get no relief; large movement can trigger nausea or vestibular symptoms.
findings.fix: Add `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` that drops transform-based motion while keeping opacity/colour transitions.

findings.selector: body > main.container > section.section > div.sk-card:nth-of-type(2) > div:nth-of-type(1) > div.skeleton.sk-avatar:nth-of-type(1)
findings.title: Weak built-in easing on an entrance
findings.detail: `linear` is too soft for a deliberate entrance. Built-in CSS easings barely accelerate.
findings.why: A stronger curve reads as intentional and responsive rather than generic.
findings.standard: Built-in CSS easings are too weak — use strong custom curves.

findings.title: Hover motion isn't gated for touch
findings.detail: Hover-triggered motion isn't wrapped in `@media (hover: hover) and (pointer: fine)`.
findings.why: Touch devices fire a synthetic hover on tap, so the animation plays on every touch — a false positive the user never intended.
findings.fix: Gate hover animations behind `@media (hover: hover) and (pointer: fine)`.
findings.standard: Gate hover motion behind (hover: hover) and (pointer: fine).
